item.author: kylepollock

item.title: 使用 fswatch 构建 Rails 脚本观察程序/运行程序 | Build a Rails script watcher/runner using fswatch
item.description:
埃里克·贝瑞

发表于我经常发现自己花费大量时间在 Rails 控制台测试代码。 随着我的示例变得越来越复杂，我经常会创建一个临时 ruby​​ 文件来编写脚本，然后复制粘贴到控制台中。

虽然这工作正常，但它很乏味并且可以优化。

例如，如果我有一个名为runme.rb的 ruby​​ 文件，并且里面有一些调试代码：

我可以使用rails runner runme.rb运行该脚本。 这很好，但是每次修改文件时我都必须返回终端。 如果我们可以自动化呢？

它将监视您指定的任何文件，然后在更改时运行脚本。

让我们在 Rails 应用程序中设置一个脚本，以便在文件更改时自动运行该文件。

安装 fswatch

安装 fswatch 很容易。 在 Mac 上，您可以运行

设置观察者脚本

我在bin/console-runner创建了一个可执行的 bash 脚本：

 #!/bin/bash usage () { echo "Watch a ruby script and use Rails runner each time it changes" echo "" echo "For example, you may have a file called \` runme.rb \` that you would" echo "run using Rails runner (eg \` rails runner runme.rb \` ). This script" echo "will watch the file and run it each time there is a change." echo "" echo "Usage:" echo " ./script/console-runner <ruby-script>" } if [ $# -eq 0 ] ; then usage exit 1 else fswatch -0 -o $1 | xargs -0 -n1 -I {} rails runner $1 fi

确保使脚本可执行：

 $ chmod +x bin/console-runner

我们可以通过运行以下命令来测试脚本文档：

 $ ./bin/console-runner Watch a ruby script and use Rails runner each time it changes For example, you may have a file called ` runme.rb ` that you would run using Rails runner ( eg ` rails runner runme.rb ` ) . This script will watch the file and run it each time there is a change. Usage: ./bin/console-runner <ruby-script>

创建要观看的控制台脚本

接下来，我在 Rails 应用程序的根文件夹中创建了一个名为runme.rb的文件。

 puts Member . count puts Rails . env

运行脚本

最后，我可以运行脚本：

 ./bin/console-runner runme.rb

成功！ 现在每次我修改runme.rb ，脚本都会重新运行。


您可以在Settings中更改默认字体。

约翰·凯文·巴鲁约 - 一旦暂停，coderberry 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，coderberry 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，coderberry 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 coderberry 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 coderberry 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 coderberry 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Eric Berry

Posted on Sep 14

I often find myself spending a lot of time in the Rails console testing out code. As my examples get more complex, I often will create a temporary ruby file to write the script, then copy-paste into the console.

Although this works ok, it is tedious and could be optimized.

Rails runner (rails r) will allow you to run a ruby script within the context of the application environment.

For example, if I had a ruby file named runme.rb and inside it had some debugging code:

I could run that script using rails runner runme.rb. This is nice, but I then have to return to the terminal each time I modify the file. What if we could automate this?

fswatch is a cross-platform file change monitor. It will watch any files you specify, then run a script on change.

Let's set up a script within our Rails app to run this file automatically when it changes.

Install fswatch

It's easy to install fswatch. On a mac, you can run

Set up a watcher script

I created an executable bash script at bin/console-runner:

#!/bin/bash usage() { echo "Watch a ruby script and use Rails runner each time it changes" echo "" echo "For example, you may have a file called \`runme.rb\` that you would" echo "run using Rails runner (e.g. \`rails runner runme.rb\`). This script" echo "will watch the file and run it each time there is a change." echo "" echo "Usage:" echo " ./script/console-runner <ruby-script>"
} if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then usage exit 1
else fswatch -0 -o $1 | xargs -0 -n1 -I{} rails runner $1
fi

Be sure to make the script executable:

$ chmod +x bin/console-runner

We can test the script documentation by running the command:

$ ./bin/console-runner
Watch a ruby script and use Rails runner each time it changes For example, you may have a file called `runme.rb` that you would
run using Rails runner (e.g. `rails runner runme.rb`). This script
will watch the file and run it each time there is a change.

Usage:
  ./bin/console-runner <ruby-script>

Create your console script to be watched

Next, I created a file in the root folder of my Rails app called runme.rb.

puts Member.count
puts Rails.env

Run the script

Finally, I can run the script:

./bin/console-runner runme.rb

Success! Now each time I modify runme.rb, the script will re-run.

 
You can change your default font in Settings.

John Kevin Baluyot - Jan 30

Once suspended, coderberry will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, coderberry will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by coderberry will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If coderberry is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag coderberry:

coderberry consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging coderberry will restore default visibility to their posts.
